title: Cheese and chive scones
subtitle: Savoury cheddar scones with fresh chives
excerpt: Ten savoury scones packed with grated mature cheddar and fresh chives, with a little mustard in the dough for heat. Ready in 30 minutes. Best eaten warm from the oven, split and buttered, or alongside soup.
sourceNotes: Cheese scones are documented in British household cookery from at least the late nineteenth century. Recipes appear in pre-1928 British domestic science textbooks and in household cookery columns in the British Newspaper Archive. The addition of chives is attested in pre-1928 British regional cookery writing; the use of English mustard with cheese is documented as a standard British flavour combination in pre-1928 household guides.

recipe.freezeNotes: Freeze baked and cooled. Reheat in a 180°C oven for 8 minutes from frozen.
recipe.makeAheadNotes: Mix the dry ingredients a day ahead. Grate the cheese ahead. Rub in the butter and continue on the day.

baking.bakeTemperatureNote: fan oven. Conventional 230°C. High heat gives the best rise and colour. They should be well browned on top in 12 to 15 minutes.

glossaryTerms.definition: Scone dough must be handled as little as possible once the liquid is added. Overworking develops gluten and produces a tough, rubbery crumb instead of the characteristic short, crumbly flake. Mix until just combined, then turn out and pat to thickness; do not knead. When cutting, press the cutter straight down and pull it straight back up without twisting: twisting seals the sides and prevents the scone from rising evenly. Place on the tray touching each other slightly to encourage upward rather than outward spread.

content.text: Cheese and chive scones are a savoury variation on the standard scone method: cold butter rubbed into flour, liquid added quickly, minimal mixing, high oven. The key is
: the less you work the dough once the milk goes in, the better the crumb. Ten scones, ready in 30 minutes.

items.prepNote: mature, grated, plus a little extra for the top

content.text: Heat the oven to 220°C fan (230°C conventional). Line a baking tray. Sift the flour, baking powder, salt, and mustard powder if using into a large bowl. Add the cold butter and rub it in with your fingertips until the mixture resembles coarse breadcrumbs. Stir in the grated cheese and chives.

Pour in the cold milk all at once and stir with a round-bladed knife until the dough just comes together in rough clumps. Do not overwork. Turn out onto a lightly floured surface and pat to 2.5 cm thick; do not roll with a rolling pin. Cut out rounds with a 6 cm cutter, pressing straight down without twisting. Gather the trimmings, pat again, and cut. Place on the tray, brush with beaten egg, and scatter a little extra cheese on top. Bake for 12 to 15 minutes, until well risen and deep golden.
